description: A quick web app to recommend video games based off of Replayability & Value, Story and Narrative, Gameplay Mechanics & Design, Presentation & Art Direction, and Technical Stability & Performance.

criteria.name: Replayability & Value
criteria.description: How many hours of gameplay are you getting relative to the price?

criteria.name: Story & Narrative
criteria.description: Is the game's story compelling? If any characters present how deep is their development? How is the game's pacing?

criteria.name: Gameplay Mechanics & Design
criteria.description: How rich the gameplay mechanics are of the game? Does the game feel responsive? How accessible is the game?

criteria.name: Presentation & Art Direction
criteria.description: How appealing is the art style? How visually appealing is the user interface? How is the music?

criteria.name: Technical Stability & Performance
criteria.description: How many game-breaking bugs/glitches are in the game? Does the game maintain at least 60 FPS?

alternatives.name: Turing Complete
alternatives.description: You will be challenged through a series of puzzles, to discover the path from NAND gates to arithmetic, memory and all the way to full CPU architectures. You will understand how programming concepts like if statements, loops and functions actually work in assembly and hardware.

alternatives.name: ULTRAKILL
alternatives.description: A fast-paced ultra-violent retro FPS that combines the skill-based style scoring of character action games with the unadulterated carnage inspired by the best shooters of the '90s.

alternatives.name: Don't Starve Together
alternatives.description: Fight, Farm, Build and Explore Together in the standalone multiplayer expansion to the uncompromising wilderness survival game, Don't Starve.

alternatives.name: Portal 2
alternatives.description: Players will explore never-before-seen areas of the Aperture Science Labs and be reunited with GLaDOS, the occasionally murderous computer companion who guided them through the original game.

alternatives.name: Tabletop Simulator
alternatives.description: Create your own original games, import custom assets, automate games with scripting, set up complete RPG dungeons, manipulate the physics, create hinges & joints, and of course flip the table when you are losing the game. The possibilities are endless!

alternatives.name: Minecraft
alternatives.description: A game made up of blocks, creatures, and community. You can survive the night or build a work of art – the choice is all yours. But if the thought of exploring a vast new world all on your own feels overwhelming, then fear not! Let’s explore what Minecraft is all about!

alternatives.name: StarCraft
alternatives.description: StarCraft introduced many revolutionary new features that have since become staples of the real-time strategy genre: asymmetric factions, meticulous balance, and a strong emphasis on deep strategy and high accessibility.
